fields.description:
## Why
Every prompt/detector change in this epic must be measured, or we repeat dso's mistake of uncalibrated
drift. This is the gating foundation: no prompt story merges without a passing eval.

## What
FIRST extend the eval harness to support code-review prompts, THEN author specs:
- Harness prerequisite (grounded gap from plan review): eval_solver.run_case() has NO execution arm for
  code-review prompt ids (code-review-base, code-review-verify, overlay ids), and eval_scorers.py REGISTRY
  has no scorer validating a code_review_* output. Add a run_case arm + register code-review gating scorers
  BEFORE specs can run.
- OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT (pin this to avoid a vacuous-pass bug): the code-review arm runs INDIVIDUAL prompts
  (base/overlays return {findings:[...]}; verify returns {verifications:[...]}) — the per-prompt calibration
  granularity, NOT the full gate. Code-review scorers therefore score on FINDINGS presence/absence (recall =
  the target defect's finding is present; no-fire = absent), NOT on the completion-verifier FAIL vocabulary.
  eval_scorers._fired() currently matches only verdict=='FAIL'; do NOT route code-review cases through it.
  If (and only if) a case exercises the full-gate verdict, extend _fired to also match verdict=='BLOCK' and
  add 'block' to FIRE_EXPECTS so dataset validation accepts it.
- Then build eval specs for base/overlays/verify with two families seeded from dso's corpus:
  NO-FIRE (must not flag): sourced-bash functions, subdirectory scripts, list-form subprocess, Rule-5
  structural greps, moved-code renames with callers updated, already-public config reads, valid
  observable-postcondition tests.
  SEEDED-DEFECT recall (must flag): tautological test, dangling deletion, asymmetric signature change,
  hallucinated API, VCS conflict marker, ticket scope drift.
- A deterministic scorer GATES; an llm-judge only REPORTS.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py (new code-review arm, findings-based output), eval_scorers.py (new
findings-based code-review scorers), src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (specs + fixtures). No gate behavior change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] eval_solver.run_case executes code-review prompt ids (base, verify, overlays) returning the prompt's
      native structured output ({findings}/{verifications}), NOT the full-gate verdict.
- [ ] >=1 code_review_* gating scorer registered that scores on findings presence/absence (not FAIL/BLOCK
      verdict); code-review recall cases cannot vacuously pass through the FAIL-only _fired.
- [ ] Eval specs exist for base, verify, and at least the tests + security overlays.
- [ ] No-fire and recall case families seeded from the dso incidents above.
- [ ] The nit-framing experiment is encoded as a committed eval case (fixture + scorer assertion); its
      no-fire/recall expectations pass on the current gate as baseline.
